clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Every single gift, no matter the size, fuels this must-win fight. grateful,Vivek Ramaswamy the election were held today, we 'd come up just short.We can't afford to lose this ground -- not now, not after everything we've built.Your support right now makes the difference between a comeback and a collapse.**| | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201 -

clean_body: | | | Peter, If you just opened this email, I hope you read this message very carefully. This is Representative Bill Huizenga. The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) is launching an all-out FUNDRAISING BLITZ against my campaign, flooding my district with MILLIONS of dollars in a desperate attempt to buy this seat and silence YOUR voice. Why would they target me in Michigan? Because they know that if Republicans like you and me LOSE this battleground seat in battleground Michigan ... Our Republican majority in the House will CRUMBLE – and with it CRASHES DOWN President Trump’s entire America First agenda. Peter , we already know the Democrats will do anything to SEIZE ABSOLUTE POWER and OBLITERATE your voice in Congress... The Democrat lobbyists would love nothing more than to INSTALL another puppet politician. The Far-Left Extremists would love to take us back to the Pelosi-Biden years of OPEN BORDERS , DEVASTATING CRIME , and SKYROCKETING PRICES. That’s why the DCCC is spending MILLIONS to attack my character, my voting record, and the conservative values you and I stand for. Yes, they’ve got the entire D.C. fundraising machine behind them. Yes, they have an entire army of lawyers and campaign consultants on speed dial... But what they do NOT have is the support of real American voters. And that’s what matters most. I’m counting on YOU to help defend my critical battleground seat by rushing over $5. Seriously, just $5. Your support doesn’t go unnoticed. Thank you, Rep. Bill Huizenga | | PAID FOR BY HUIZENGA FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 245 Zeeland, MI, 49464

body:
Washington voters just rejected Big Oil's most expensive play.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119353264/582778049/1328794144?refcode=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjMyZmFlNTFmLTRhYzItZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Iqfv9deE-PNKVU-_MyV-VsUUXF5U9J1TIRLaM2G6IN4=&emci=bb897819-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)
---
There's a moment in every David vs. Goliath fight when the giant realizes they're going to lose. For Big Oil, that moment came on Election Night in Washington State. **State Senators Victoria Hunt and Deb Krishnadasan won their highly competitive races despite a super PAC bankrolled by Chevron, the Koch family, and other big business interests dumping _more than $500,000_ to unseat our endorsed climate champions.** **[This is what victory looks like when people like you rise up and refuse to let corporate polluters buy our elections. Before I share more about why this matters, can I count on you to chip in to my Climate PAC before the end of today so we can make more wins like these possible?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119353265/582778050/2105508926?refcode=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjMyZmFlNTFmLTRhYzItZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Iqfv9deE-PNKVU-_MyV-VsUUXF5U9J1TIRLaM2G6IN4=)** | [**DONATE NOW**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119353266/582778051/2105508926?refcode=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjMyZmFlNTFmLTRhYzItZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Iqfv9deE-PNKVU-_MyV-VsUUXF5U9J1TIRLaM2G6IN4=)
---
🎉 **Victoria Hunt faced one of the most competitive races in Washington.** Republican leadership and Big Oil saw her district as their best chance at flipping a Senate seat and chipping away at the Democratic majority. They came at her with everything they had — misinformation, attack ads, and corporate cash. But Victoria stayed focused on what matters: modernizing Washington's Clean Energy Transformation Act, protecting public lands, fighting for clean air, and delivering for her community. Voters saw through the attacks and chose the leader who works for them, not corporate polluters.
---
🎉 **Deb Krishnadasan withstood what she called _"one of the most expensive waves of corporate-funded attack ads in history"_ — funded in part by $1.3 million of independent spending.** Even as the attacks piled up, she never wavered from her message: holding polluters accountable, advancing clean energy solutions, and pushing Washington toward 100% clean energy. And voters rewarded that courage.
---
This is exactly why we endorse leaders like Victoria and Deb around the country. They're not intimidated by corporate money, political bullies, or Big Oil's attack machine. They refuse to back down, and no amount of spending can silence leaders who stand up for what's right.
That’s what’s possible when this movement comes together, when we invest early, and when we prioritize state and local races where climate action can’t wait another second.
**[Now we need to carry this momentum forward because Big Oil isn't giving up. They'll keep trying to buy seats in the midterms and beyond._Our_ job is to keep proving that people power beats their money every single time. That’s why I’m humbly asking for your early support today so my team can start preparing for the tough, must-win fights ahead, nationwide.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119353267/582778052/2105508926?refcode=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.32fae51f-4ac2-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjMyZmFlNTFmLTRhYzItZjAxMS04MTk2LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Iqfv9deE-PNKVU-_MyV-VsUUXF5U9J1TIRLaM2G6IN4=)**

clean_body: Washington voters just rejected Big Oil's most expensive play. | There's a moment in every David vs. Goliath fight when the giant realizes they're going to lose. For Big Oil, that moment came on Election Night in Washington State. State Senators Victoria Hunt and Deb Krishnadasan won their highly competitive races despite a super PAC bankrolled by Chevron, the Koch family, and other big business interests dumping more than $500,000 to unseat our endorsed climate champions. = | = 🎉 Victoria Hunt faced one of the most competitive races in Washington. Republican leadership and Big Oil saw her district as their best chance at flipping a Senate seat and chipping away at the Democratic majority. They came at her with everything they had — misinformation, attack ads, and corporate cash. But Victoria stayed focused on what matters: modernizing Washington's Clean Energy Transformation Act, protecting public lands, fighting for clean air, and delivering for her community. Voters saw through the attacks and chose the leader who works for them, not corporate polluters. 🎉 Deb Krishnadasan withstood what she called "one of the most expensive waves of corporate-funded attack ads in history" — funded in part by $1.3 million of independent spending. Even as the attacks piled up, she never wavered from her message: holding polluters accountable, advancing clean energy solutions, and pushing Washington toward 100% clean energy. And voters rewarded that courage. This is exactly why we endorse leaders like Victoria and Deb around the country. They're not intimidated by corporate money, political bullies, or Big Oil's attack machine. They refuse to back down, and no amount of spending can silence leaders who stand up for what's right. That’s what’s possible when this movement comes together, when we invest early, and when we prioritize state and local races where climate action can’t wait another second. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for being a part of this fight. xx Jane = | Follow along: | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

subject: The National Guard is not Trump’s Royal Guard.

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
AG Rob Bonta here with an update for you on our National Guard lawsuit.
After Trump used an illegal scheme to federalize the California National Guard and deploy troops to the Los Angeles area back in June, Angelenos are still living under an unnecessary military presence — with no end in sight.
The Trump administration is essentially holding California’s National Guard hostage, repeatedly extending their federalization and deployment without any basis or justification.
This week I renewed my calls to the District Court to take swift action in our case:
→ End the extended and needless federalization of California National Guard troops.
→ Send our hardworking servicemembers back to their regular duty and regular lives.
→ Free Los Angeles from a completely unjustified military occupation.
The National Guard is not Trump’s Royal Guard to use as a personal, traveling police force whenever he wants, wherever he wants, for however long he wants.
As California’s Attorney General, I won’t back down from this fight — the President can’t be allowed to unlawfully infringe on Californians’ rights with impunity.
Please join us today and help power the work to hold Trump accountable and free our cities of military occupation. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25?express_lane=true&amount=25] DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25?express_lane=true&amount=50]

DONATE $500 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25?express_lane=true&amount=500] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
I will never stop fighting to defend your rights and freedoms from an administration hell-bent on trampling them.
Thanks for being a part of this team.
—Rob
Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

subject: Charlie showed us how to WIN
body:

Fellow Patriot,Charlie Kirk knew something long before the rest of America caught on. That the next generation of young Americans were desperate for** ** truth... That the Left's grip on the culture wasn't as strong as it looked... And with boldness, hard work, and a focused strategy -- WE COULD WIN. Charlie didn't just talk about it. _He built a movement that's changed history._ **And long before he left us... he wrote ****_How We Win_**** -- the playbook that lays it all out.**

name: Jack Schlossberg
subject: I'm running for Congress!
body:
I know you’re likely getting a ton of emails from candidates and causes right now, Peter.
But I have to ask: Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $25 between Real Texas PAC and my Senate campaign in Ohio? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
What if I told you that right after I joined this race, experts at The Cook Political Report increased our odds of winning?
What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me?
What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you?
This grassroots, small-dollar donor movement is our best chance to flip Ohio’s Senate seat. But we’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to win.
So please, split $25 or more between Real Texas PAC and me to power our campaign forward, win in Ohio, and keep up the fight for working people. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Sherrod Brown and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?express_lane=true&refcode=101725_2&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2&amount=25]
Thank you so much,
Sherrod Brown
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]

Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/Pq6mZqR8vUu32u6MFdTiQw2?unsubscribedata=m%2BLCLQ5s%2FTViFHlIXX8J9COiXNs1ydMoVH0WuuQWha4Yo3LPx9UhCmYcWjQRM1PFwaRgLheAZ2KPZSxL4FXRMWQR6zcX%2F8xH48dCVOMq87RRGvqmxY%2F38PtG2m3NSarT%2BoU3K4nq9Dpsclvl8aEXqRwelTHQ5JBJXF3l5nSErnBdAqX245GZT0kYepIVM50Ys8p3gRBI%2Fp5kLa1nt7lpT1RDQW7%2Bvs0Bz0fSnuNEu84Fsso62pRX%2Fl1Sj2jHdh3xnb4A%2BFbuC1KEkIv6b53u%2B%2BN5Kwl7k3mIDDe6%2FXWxAPcgQ%2BPyyhBqgfhU5S%2B6ZoCzMo99qbw9bAxRcudwEc8wiBr7VRMVN0eTTdLbX2SDzPNII5GK9ptyZC%2BV1zoNNWrUou9gezPgWPnqwPNKgohXWSihESZAT3b%2B%2BN3CzrS66os%3D .

name: Adam Schiff
subject: The sky won’t fall but…
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| We need to catch up if we want to flip the Senate and show the strength of our movement to defend our democracy against President Trump.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9193%2E5257348%2EuxyfbA)
I won't keep you long, Derek, but today is our mid-quarter benchmark, and we're slightly behind on our fundraising goal.
That is fine, the sky won't fall — but we do need to catch up if we want to flip the Senate and show the strength of our movement to defend our democracy against President Trump.
Can you pitch in before midnight tonight to help us catch up? Here is the link to give: [**secure.actblue.com/donate/q42025_abs_em**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20251115_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=9193%2E5257348%2EuxyfbA)
I really do appreciate anything you can pitch in. I understand that these are very difficult times, and your support does not go unnoticed.
Thank you,

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Democrats caved.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

This week, eight corporate Senate Democrats caved and voted to surrender
our healthcare to Republicans who plan to raise premiums for millions of
Americans to pay for Trump’s billionaire tax cuts.

Democrats held their ground for 40 days — the longest government shutdown
in American history — to stop health insurance premiums from skyrocketing
even higher than they already are for millions of working people.

Then, these eight corporate Democrats folded... for nothing in return. And
Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer let them.

It’s time for Chuck Schumer to step down as the Democratic leader in the
Senate.

This is absolutely pathetic behavior by the so-called opposition party.
Voters expected Democrats to hold the line for their basic healthcare and
food benefits — instead, they surrendered.

Every Senate Democrat who joined Republicans to pass this resolution sold
the American people out, and we should make sure they have no future in
public office.

This is what Justice Democrats has been saying from the beginning: we
don’t just need more Democrats in office — we need champions for
working-class communities who will actually fight for the people.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251115.3zjjdE&refcode=em20251115-37196&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37196%2E1203867%2EH9K8yV ]Help us clean house in the Democratic Party and support progressive,
working-class candidates taking on corporate stooges in next year’s
primary elections: rush $5 or more now!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251115.3zjjdE&refcode=em20251115-37196&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37196%2E1203867%2EH9K8yV ]Contribute $5

Voting blue no matter who isn’t enough,
Peter. It’s time we make that clear to the
feckless Democrats leading our party and make room for real fighters.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251115-37196-footer&t=1022&refcode2=37196_1203867_H9K8yV&akid=37196%2E1203867%2EH9K8yV

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=37196%2E1203867%2EH9K8yV

If there is one thing that AI does. It is do things **AT SPEED**. It can write at speed, it can code at speed, it can make beautiful art at speed, it can put people out of work at speed and it also appears that it can hurtle towards a financial disaster at speed.
In the future when we look back this will likely be the moment that people will say that the writing was on the wall. We didn't realize it in that moment, but this was when AI crossed the rubicon.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/db2de771-7295-4b65-b1eb-b43b174c56b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In the last few months at the Capitalist, we have started to flag some of the very thorny issues that were on the horizon for AI, but events have moved forward at such a rapid pace that they are no longer on the horizon.
In June we wrote about the **[power issues facing AI.](https://substack.com/redirect/6c57eec2-c6dd-46f0-91e7-0bd724cc2654?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **In early October we flagged the **[staggering debt and financing issues facing AI.](https://substack.com/redirect/a50162a4-b2ff-4b98-879d-aa5d28862e7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **In late October we called attention to the **[revenue generation of AI (or rather the lack of it)](https://substack.com/redirect/81c989b0-17f7-4bd5-a036-07d2621be08e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Now, barely five months since we started, in the second week of November, these issues have exploded into the mainstream.
The AI Utopia envisioned in 2024 is now dealing with the cold reality of late 2025. Whispered mutterings consigned to the dark corners of the internet have transformed into serious **[questions](https://substack.com/redirect/0619ccb3-b5fd-4995-a4ef-8361320be954?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , asked by serious **[people](https://substack.com/redirect/0619ccb3-b5fd-4995-a4ef-8361320be954?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with serious **[projections](https://substack.com/redirect/0619ccb3-b5fd-4995-a4ef-8361320be954?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and none of them look great.
What is coming in to focus with AI is three distinct issues:
* **There isn 't the power to fuel the AI Utopia. **
* **There isn 't enough money to invest in building the AI Utopia. **
* **The revenue being generated by AI is far from Utopia-esque.**

## Power to the people! (And not for data centers.)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6cb7414b-bbaa-4702-ac8a-570d307fe29e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[The power needed](https://substack.com/redirect/6c57eec2-c6dd-46f0-91e7-0bd724cc2654?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to run AI is on a scale that we can **[barely comprehend.](https://substack.com/redirect/a50162a4-b2ff-4b98-879d-aa5d28862e7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** America's grid was strained before AI was even a functional reality. A Texas winter storm or a California heatwave could easily put it on its back. AI is only in its infancy now - we are not even close to AI Utopia yet - and its appetite is already becoming a major headache both in terms of **[feeding it](https://substack.com/redirect/d2826970-10a0-4513-b014-4b0afa7c9c51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** but also politically. At town halls across the nation, questions are beginning to bubble up about why vast percentages of states electricity reserves are being gobbled up by data centers, **[driving prices higher](https://substack.com/redirect/7a318fe2-4455-4eff-84a7-0ba1d19b4eb6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** than they already are for cash strapped residents.
The ever more fantastical AI deals being announced almost daily promise a glittering future but do nothing to address the bottom line which is that the resources needed to power the "glittering future" just do not exist yet.
It's also not just power. The ever ascending climb of AI Chips in the power stakes means that very soon "cooling" and "thermal management" of data centers will be the next goldmine industry. Air cooling will just not cut it anymore. It won't be long before the drain of data centers will not just be power but water as well, as AI titans seek to quench the white hot fires that they have built. For anyone wanting a preview of what that will look like you only need to look at the bitter fight that has raged over **[who gets to use the waters of the Colorado River](https://substack.com/redirect/65fcf91d-6b1f-4382-b20c-189679cedeef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for the last century. It will be coming to a town hall near you ..... shortly.
**Money Money Money (but from who?)**
It is no secret to anyone that there has been an AI boom. The news is filled near daily with stories of billion dollar deals being inked, investments being made, and shortly after news breaks of a deal or an investment there is another story about how much **[XYZ company's stock has soared](https://substack.com/redirect/a50162a4-b2ff-4b98-879d-aa5d28862e7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** off the back of the deal.
A lot of these are multi year deals to either build data centers, or supply chips to data centers, or to invest in the companies supplying the chips or data centers or both. However as we have noted previously, a lot of these deals are opaque and near circular in nature. You have AI companies investing in chip manufacturers and then buying chips from those manufacturers which causes stocks in one or both parties to rise. To paraphrase from Jeff Foxworthy _**" If your company investment tree does not fork, you might be an AI hyperscaler."**_
This investment carousel makes it hard to pin down exactly how much is being invested, to who, and by who. There are questions starting to be raised by some that **[behind the fanfare](https://substack.com/redirect/62c294eb-d029-454b-a168-e3a420ba3aa9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , there maybe an investment shortfall between what actually exists and what it will take to fulfill these deals (_dreams?_). Quite how large that is, nobody really knows but some have **[estimated it to be as much as $1.4 trillion dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/0219de01-10b3-4830-a0bd-28c93cc88778?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Were this to be the case, the question would be **who fills that gap?**

The obvious answer would be the Federal government, because AI has moved on from just a business proposition. There is now a national security dimension to winning the "AI War." It is already being speculated by some that the US and China are in a **[Cold War over AI specifically](https://substack.com/redirect/7143e62f-21d8-4dff-aada-00a9565ada40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ,** and tech in general.
President Trump has already made it clear with his investments in rare earth mineral mines, the federal acquisition of TikTok and the export restrictions on Nvidia relating to exporting its most advanced chips going to China, that the US is intent on[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/01fed98a-173a-4088-9c3f-cc66e5c32eab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[fortifying and expanding on its AI, data, and rare earth security.](https://substack.com/redirect/01fed98a-173a-4088-9c3f-cc66e5c32eab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** However, restricting sales of cutting edge computer chips and prospecting for rare earth minerals is a long way from **[footing the bill to fund a hyperscaler CEO's AI Utopian dreams.](https://substack.com/redirect/b96dc079-e8dd-41d7-8ce0-104755a1dfae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/b96dc079-e8dd-41d7-8ce0-104755a1dfae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)So it looks like any shortfall in funds will have to come from somewhere else.
Which leads us to...
### Build AI ✅ \- Upgrade AI ✅ \- ????? - Profit....(maybe?)
The power issues described above are a **[bottleneck](https://substack.com/redirect/ca034d6f-a6d0-40ea-8bfb-3cbfd8de2883?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to AI advancement, but they "could" be solved eventually even if there was some time lag. The investment holes "could" be plugged even if it was through unpopular means.
Those issues on a macroeconomic level (in a no-holds-barred-fantasyland,) "could" be resolved. But the AI revenue question makes these issues look like child's play.
JP Morgan has highlighted this 800 pound gorilla issue in one of its latest reports perfectly.
_**" Big Picture, to drive a 10% return on our modeled AI investments through 2030 would require $650 billion of annual revenue into perpetuity which is an astonishingly large number. But for context that equates to 58bp of Global GDP, or $34.72 per month from every single iPhone user, or $180 per month from every NFLX subscriber"**_**** \- JPM
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b811666f-c009-4090-b4e6-2f69e6466611?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The scale of this issue cannot be understated.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4a7af9e4-9d5e-4569-9b0d-e14ea5542c27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
There are more than 8 billion people in the world. 1.56 billion of them have an iPhone. For the Hyperscaler's AI gamble to work, according to JP Morgan, every, single, last one of them would need to pony up $40 a month for at least the next 5 years and probably longer.
Logic alone says that is an almost impossibly tall order, but when combined with the other issues we've noted above this gets in to the realm of near impossible.
Because, quite simply put, there is no compelling reason that exists today for why 1.56 billion people need to spend $40 a month, every month, on AI as it exists at the moment. Maybe that changes in the future, it could well do.
**But ask yourself this:** How many of your friends do you know who pay for AI? If they have an iPhone, ask them. If they don't, that's a problem for the AI industry.
Prospective AI subscription buyers are facing credit card delinquencies and auto loan defaults that are at their highest in history. Post-Covid Inflation in its broadest definition has touched nearly every facet of peoples lives from housing, to food and energy costs. The failures of the past be it the 2008 bailouts and the ensuing money printing, or the disaster of Obamacare that will likely see healthcare premiums skyrocket, are all weighing on the very people who the AI industry needs to go out and pay an extra $40 a month.
The people don't have it, and even if they did have $40 a month to spare, why would you buy an AI subscription? (To fulfill Sam Altman's AI Utopian dreams is likely not near the top of their list.)
## And so it begins
If there was a moment where, in the future, you will be able to look back to and pinpoint the moment when the wheels came off the AI bandwagon (or at least started to wobble) it will likely be OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar's **[fumbled statement](https://substack.com/redirect/90009b63-64a8-4547-b53e-5cf754df8b92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** about a government providing **[federal guarantees](https://substack.com/redirect/58381724-30ee-4673-9097-29707a09eeaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to OpenAI's investments.
Coming so soon after the announcement of OpenAI's multi billion dollar deal with Oracle, the statement landed like a lead balloon.
Backlash was swift with even President Trump's AI Czar David Sacks **[making a statement](https://substack.com/redirect/1cc4a588-0294-4d88-ad1d-646102923786?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that there was _**" no federal bailout for AI."**_ His statement went further and for OpenAI it was a real problem. Posting on X, Sacks said _**" The U.S. has at least 5 major frontier model companies. If one fails, others will take its place,"**_
In so many words what Sacks said was basically that "There are a bunch of big players in the AI game and if some of them go bankrupt along the way, thats fine, the market will pick a winner and we're not in the business of picking winners and losers."
For the AI industry in general and OpenAI in particular, this was the starting pistol for unleashing two years worth of pent up skepticism about AI, and it came thick and fast.
In a frantic backpedal of a statement Sarah Friar tried to explain that she wasn't asking for a "pre-bailout" but it didn't work.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/34abc364-1e21-47f6-9f37-e1ccda38173d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Sam Altman **[followed up](https://substack.com/redirect/e0c258b4-2e01-4377-a787-56f565bca13c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with a Bill Ackman style essay on X later but the damage was done.
Suddenly all those massive deals were cast under a different light because, even if it wasn't the case, it looked to many like OpenAI was making deals with the idea in the back of their mind that if they grew fast enough and became big enough they would be backstopped by the government because they would be "too big to fail."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d20ccfb8-1fd6-456b-a505-3b8951dce060?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As we noted above, it is certainly true that AI now has a national security dimension, and that the AI **["Cold War"](https://substack.com/redirect/7143e62f-21d8-4dff-aada-00a9565ada40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with China means that the government has a vested interest to seeing America lead and ultimately win the AI race. However, in light of OpenAI's botched handling of the "backstop" comments, it does appear that in the minds of some Hyperscalers, they have conflated Uncle Sam's need for a robust AI industry with their personal zealotry to achieve AI Utopia at any price.
#### The boundless optimism is gone and now come the questions.
In the heady days of late 2024 no one asked how AI was going to make money, people were just amazed to see it work, but now they are asking and it is not going well. The initial awe surrounding AI has dissipated, people now want to know, _" When is there going to be comparable revenue to cost, where is this financing coming from, how (who) is going to fund these mind boggling investments if 1.56 billion people don't put their hand in their pocket and pay $40 a month?"_
Sam Altman was pushed on this very subject in an interview with Brad Gerstner on the Bg2Pod and he became visibly defensive, **[snapping](https://substack.com/redirect/9e1b9da2-1495-4f56-92be-7ad23be5f9ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
_**" If you want to sell your shares, I'll find you a buyer,"**_
Altman's defensive attitude did not play well amongst industry watchers.
It was not just OpenAI that is getting the tough questions. The AI industry in general has also been subject to a lot more scrutiny than it has been in the last 12 months. Instead of a non-stop stream of good news about "everything AI" there are now more than a few pieces of bad news.
Meta having spent billions on its own AI projects was **[put under the microscope](https://substack.com/redirect/5ad33624-d465-4dad-b9c9-5dc95919ba9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after its Q3 results were announced. Mark Zuckerberg put up a vigorous defense of why Meta was spending as much as they were on AI but had to admit that they will likely have to spend even more in 2026. Investors were not happy and Meta's shares **[tanked as a result.](https://substack.com/redirect/5ad33624-d465-4dad-b9c9-5dc95919ba9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
**[Coreweave](https://substack.com/redirect/bed061b8-164a-4410-8992-b8be9139e370?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , yet another AI company that launched its IPO in 2025, took a beating when CNBC's Jim Cramer pushed its CEO Mike Intrator to break down details of his company's investment strategy and the CEO sidestepped the questions. The market punished Coreweave with a **[20% drop in its share price.](https://substack.com/redirect/bed061b8-164a-4410-8992-b8be9139e370?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a1b99ff2-8de5-47f0-9442-91ea7d846403?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Oracle, fresh off its blockbuster deal with Open AI and several surges in its share price, is **[now being scrutinized](https://substack.com/redirect/046b67db-fde1-401b-aa08-67ee785841aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** over the debt load that it is taking on.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/93525795-7c84-4c05-a401-f48adbda8d90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Even Nvidia, once the golden child of the AI revolution, woke up to the announcement by **[SoftBank out of Japan](https://substack.com/redirect/048fbd23-e07e-4c8b-aa68-54f660ea207b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** (one of their earliest investors) that they would be liquidating their entire Nvidia holdings for $5.38 billion dollars. SoftBank was quick to point out that this had nothing to do with concerns over the AI industry and announced that they would be investing instead in OpenAI, increasing their stake from 4% to 11%. Granted, OpenAI has and will likely continue to buy a great many chips from Nvidia but this is yet more turbulence in the AI space.
The **[latest thing rocking the AI industry](https://substack.com/redirect/35b0d3f0-5057-4a93-8771-983482682230?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** is that Michael Burry of "Big Short" fame recently posted that he has taken up a massive short position against both Nvidia and Palantir, accusing them of artificially extending the lifespan of the chips in their accounting, thus giving the impression of lower depreciation costs and therefore able to book higher profits. Burry has yet to provide all of his evidence but the sheer fact that it has been voiced runs contrary to the fairytale narrative that AI has benefited from up until now.
This accusation, _if it were to be true,_ would do more than take the sheen off of AI, because it harkens back to what was going on inside the company that Nvidia replaced on the S&P500 in 2000.
**Enron.**
It is never a good thing when people **[start equating your company with Enron](https://substack.com/redirect/f0bf0563-c831-4854-860f-ee21d6567d00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[suggesting that you are the modern Jeff Skilling.](https://substack.com/redirect/7f147fed-fce8-413e-8a5a-52a247c910e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
As it stands right now there is no concrete proof yet if what Burry is claiming is true, but what is certainly true is that the AI Utopia being touted by Hyperscalers:
* **Needs more power than the grid can supply.**
* **Will shortly require vast amounts of water to cool their data centers.**
* **Produce a product that they have yet to figure out how to sell to 1.56 billion people for $40 a month.**
* **Is made up of unfulfilled deals that total more than the GDP of developed nations based on the idea that 1.56 billion people WILL pay $40 per month.**

This piece is not meant to denigrate AI or the path of progress we are on. AI, like the internet before it, will most likely take over the world one day and be as ubiquitous as the cellphone, of that we are almost certain.
But, given the state of play in the AI industry right now, with all that we have covered here, short of an earth shattering, transcendental, biblical, epoch level shift in "something" the question no one wants to ask hangs ominously in the air: Is the writing on the wall for the AI boom?
Or to put it more crudely, Does the AI Emperor have no clothes on?

"If your company investment tree does not fork, you might be an AI hyperscaler." | | | Nov 15 | | | | | | | | | | | | The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism If there is one thing that AI does. It is do things AT SPEED. It can write at speed, it can code at speed, it can make beautiful art at speed, it can put people out of work at speed and it also appears that it can hurtle towards a financial disaster at speed. In the future when we look back this will likely be the moment that people will say that the writing was on the wall. We didn't realize it in that moment, but this was when AI crossed the rubicon. | | ---|---|--- In the last few months at the Capitalist, we have started to flag some of the very thorny issues that were on the horizon for AI, but events have moved forward at such a rapid pace that they are no longer on the horizon. In June we wrote about the In early October we flagged the In late October we called attention to the Now, barely five months since we started, in the second week of November, these issues have exploded into the mainstream. The AI Utopia envisioned in 2024 is now dealing with the cold reality of late 2025. Whispered mutterings consigned to the dark corners of the internet have transformed into serious , asked by serious with serious and none of them look great. What is coming in to focus with AI is three distinct issues: There isn 't the power to fuel the AI Utopia. There isn 't enough money to invest in building the AI Utopia. The revenue being generated by AI is far from Utopia-esque. | | ---|---|--- Your body loses 1-2% of its collagen every year after 30--and it's the most abundant protein you've got. Translation? Less support for joints, skin, nails, and overall resilience. NativePath Collagen helps you restore what time chips away--clean, grass-fed, no fillers, no junk. One study found that women taking just 5g a day saw meaningful improvements in bone support over a year. That's the power of consistent collagen intake. Just mix two flavorless scoops into coffee or any drink for 18g of easy, high-quality protein that supports joints, mobility, digestion, and more. Power to the people! (And not for data centers.) | | ---|---|--- to run AI is on a scale that we can America's grid was strained before AI was even a functional reality. A Texas winter storm or a California heatwave could easily put it on its back. AI is only in its infancy now - we are not even close to AI Utopia yet - and its appetite is already becoming a major headache both in terms of but also politically. At town halls across the nation, questions are beginning to bubble up about why vast percentages of states electricity reserves are being gobbled up by data centers, than they already are for cash strapped residents. The ever more fantastical AI deals being announced almost daily promise a glittering future but do nothing to address the bottom line which is that the resources needed to power the "glittering future" just do not exist yet. It's also not just power. The ever ascending climb of AI Chips in the power stakes means that very soon "cooling" and "thermal management" of data centers will be the next goldmine industry. Air cooling will just not cut it anymore. It won't be long before the drain of data centers will not just be power but water as well, as AI titans seek to quench the white hot fires that they have built. For anyone wanting a preview of what that will look like you only need to look at the bitter fight that has raged over for the last century. It will be coming to a town hall near you ..... shortly. Money Money Money (but from who?) It is no secret to anyone that there has been an AI boom. The news is filled near daily with stories of billion dollar deals being inked, investments being made, and shortly after news breaks of a deal or an investment there is another story about how much off the back of the deal. A lot of these are multi year deals to either build data centers, or supply chips to data centers, or to invest in the companies supplying the chips or data centers or both. However as we have noted previously, a lot of these deals are opaque and near circular in nature. You have AI companies investing in chip manufacturers and then buying chips from those manufacturers which causes stocks in one or both parties to rise. To paraphrase from Jeff Foxworthy " If your company investment tree does not fork, you might be an AI hyperscaler." This investment carousel makes it hard to pin down exactly how much is being invested, to who, and by who. There are questions starting to be raised by some that , there maybe an investment shortfall between what actually exists and what it will take to fulfill these deals (dreams?). Quite how large that is, nobody really knows but some have . Were this to be the case, the question would be who fills that gap? The obvious answer would be the Federal government, because AI has moved on from just a business proposition. There is now a national security dimension to winning the "AI War." It is already being speculated by some that the US and China are in a , and tech in general. President Trump has already made it clear with his investments in rare earth mineral mines, the federal acquisition of TikTok and the export restrictions on Nvidia relating to exporting its most advanced chips going to China, that the US is intent on However, restricting sales of cutting edge computer chips and prospecting for rare earth minerals is a long way from it looks like any shortfall in funds will have to come from somewhere else. Which leads us to... Build AI ✅ \- Upgrade AI ✅ \- ????? - Profit....(maybe?) The power issues described above are a to AI advancement, but they "could" be solved eventually even if there was some time lag. The investment holes "could" be plugged even if it was through unpopular means. Those issues on a macroeconomic level (in a no-holds-barred-fantasyland,) "could" be resolved. But the AI revenue question makes these issues look like child's play. JP Morgan has highlighted this 800 pound gorilla issue in one of its latest reports perfectly. " Big Picture, to drive a 10% return on our modeled AI investments through 2030 would require $650 billion of annual revenue into perpetuity which is an astonishingly large number. But for context that equates to 58bp of Global GDP, or $34.72 per month from every single iPhone user, or $180 per month from every NFLX subscriber" \- JPM | | ---|---|--- The scale of this issue cannot be understated. | | ---|---|--- There are more than 8 billion people in the world. 1.56 billion of them have an iPhone. For the Hyperscaler's AI gamble to work, according to JP Morgan, every, single, last one of them would need to pony up $40 a month for at least the next 5 years and probably longer. Logic alone says that is an almost impossibly tall order, but when combined with the other issues we've noted above this gets in to the realm of near impossible. Because, quite simply put, there is no compelling reason that exists today for why 1.56 billion people need to spend $40 a month, every month, on AI as it exists at the moment. Maybe that changes in the future, it could well do. But ask yourself this: How many of your friends do you know who pay for AI? If they have an iPhone, ask them. If they don't, that's a problem for the AI industry. Prospective AI subscription buyers are facing credit card delinquencies and auto loan defaults that are at their highest in history. Post-Covid Inflation in its broadest definition has touched nearly every facet of peoples lives from housing, to food and energy costs. The failures of the past be it the 2008 bailouts and the ensuing money printing, or the disaster of Obamacare that will likely see healthcare premiums skyrocket, are all weighing on the very people who the AI industry needs to go out and pay an extra $40 a month. The people don't have it, and even if they did have $40 a month to spare, why would you buy an AI subscription? (To fulfill Sam Altman's AI Utopian dreams is likely not near the top of their list.) And so it begins If there was a moment where, in the future, you will be able to look back to and pinpoint the moment when the wheels came off the AI bandwagon (or at least started to wobble) it will likely be OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar's about a government providing to OpenAI's investments. Coming so soon after the announcement of OpenAI's multi billion dollar deal with Oracle, the statement landed like a lead balloon. Backlash was swift with even President Trump's AI Czar David Sacks that there was " no federal bailout for AI." His statement went further and for OpenAI it was a real problem. Posting on X, Sacks said " The U.S. has at least 5 major frontier model companies. If one fails, others will take its place," In so many words what Sacks said was basically that "There are a bunch of big players in the AI game and if some of them go bankrupt along the way, thats fine, the market will pick a winner and we're not in the business of picking winners and losers." For the AI industry in general and OpenAI in particular, this was the starting pistol for unleashing two years worth of pent up skepticism about AI, and it came thick and fast. In a frantic backpedal of a statement Sarah Friar tried to explain that she wasn't asking for a "pre-bailout" but it didn't work. | | ---|---|--- Sam Altman with a Bill Ackman style essay on X later but the damage was done. Suddenly all those massive deals were cast under a different light because, even if it wasn't the case, it looked to many like OpenAI was making deals with the idea in the back of their mind that if they grew fast enough and became big enough they would be backstopped by the government because they would be "too big to fail." | | ---|---|--- As we noted above, it is certainly true that AI now has a national security dimension, and that the AI with China means that the government has a vested interest to seeing America lead and ultimately win the AI race. However, in light of OpenAI's botched handling of the "backstop" comments, it does appear that in the minds of some Hyperscalers, they have conflated Uncle Sam's need for a robust AI industry with their personal zealotry to achieve AI Utopia at any price. The boundless optimism is gone and now come the questions. In the heady days of late 2024 no one asked how AI was going to make money, people were just amazed to see it work, but now they are asking and it is not going well. The initial awe surrounding AI has dissipated, people now want to know, " When is there going to be comparable revenue to cost, where is this financing coming from, how (who) is going to fund these mind boggling investments if 1.56 billion people don't put their hand in their pocket and pay $40 a month?" Sam Altman was pushed on this very subject in an interview with Brad Gerstner on the Bg2Pod and he became visibly defensive, . " If you want to sell your shares, I'll find you a buyer," Altman's defensive attitude did not play well amongst industry watchers. It was not just OpenAI that is getting the tough questions. The AI industry in general has also been subject to a lot more scrutiny than it has been in the last 12 months. Instead of a non-stop stream of good news about "everything AI" there are now more than a few pieces of bad news. Meta having spent billions on its own AI projects was after its Q3 results were announced. Mark Zuckerberg put up a vigorous defense of why Meta was spending as much as they were on AI but had to admit that they will likely have to spend even more in 2026. Investors were not happy and Meta's shares , yet another AI company that launched its IPO in 2025, took a beating when CNBC's Jim Cramer pushed its CEO Mike Intrator to break down details of his company's investment strategy and the CEO sidestepped the questions. The market punished Coreweave with a | | ---|---|--- Oracle, fresh off its blockbuster deal with Open AI and several surges in its share price, is over the debt load that it is taking on. | | ---|---|--- Even Nvidia, once the golden child of the AI revolution, woke up to the announcement by (one of their earliest investors) that they would be liquidating their entire Nvidia holdings for $5.38 billion dollars. SoftBank was quick to point out that this had nothing to do with concerns over the AI industry and announced that they would be investing instead in OpenAI, increasing their stake from 4% to 11%. Granted, OpenAI has and will likely continue to buy a great many chips from Nvidia but this is yet more turbulence in the AI space. The is that Michael Burry of "Big Short" fame recently posted that he has taken up a massive short position against both Nvidia and Palantir, accusing them of artificially extending the lifespan of the chips in their accounting, thus giving the impression of lower depreciation costs and therefore able to book higher profits. Will shortly require vast amounts of water to cool their data centers. Produce a product that they have yet to figure out how to sell to 1.56 billion people for $40 a month. Is made up of unfulfilled deals that total more than the GDP of developed nations based on the idea that 1.56 billion people WILL pay $40 per month. This piece is not meant to denigrate AI or the path of progress we are on. name: Team Pramila
subject: tonight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
There’s a lot going on right now.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251115-5625-footer&t=1&akid=5625%2E420207%2ED37FP2)
Peter, there's a lot going on right now:
* **Republicans are _still_ trying to stop the release of the full Epstein files**, just days after new emails show Epstein himself alleging that Trump knew about his crimes.

* **Health care costs are surging** as insurers prepare for subsidies under the Affordable Care Act to expire — and Republicans just caused the longest government shutdown in our history because they _want_ it to happen.

* **Trump just extended his National Guard deployment in Washington, DC** , with troops now scheduled to be in the capital through the end of February. At the same time, violent ICE raids are continuing around the country.

While Pramila is focused on holding the Trump administration accountable and organizing nonviolent resistance to their authoritarian agenda, our campaign is working hard to reach our $16,000 mid-month goal before tonight’s deadline.
[**Can we count on you to pitch in $3 right now to help us reach this goal and keep Pramila focused on the important work she’s doing for our progressive movement?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251115-1.cJ71N9&refcode=em20251115-5625&amount=3&t=2&akid=5625%2E420207%2ED37FP2)

subject: Midnight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
It takes millions of dollars to run and win in a state as large as California.
Peter,
I have a quick, no BS ask before midnight tonight: Can you donate $5 to our campaign to help us reach our $20,000 fundraising goal? Use this link to chip in:
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20251115_1_ak?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251115_1_ak.MIgL8i&refcode=em20251115-40423&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=40423%2E3422442%2EluAcE7)**
Let me explain why your support matters.
It takes millions of dollars to run and win in a state as large as California. We have to hire staff, buy ads, and travel across the state to meet with voters.
Campaigns are won by hitting small goals that build toward the big ones. Reaching this $20,000 benchmark before our mid-month fundraising deadline keeps us on track as this race heats up.
That’s why I’m asking directly: will you chip in $5 or more using this link before midnight?
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20251115_1_ak?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251115_1_ak.MIgL8i&refcode=em20251115-40423&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=40423%2E3422442%2EluAcE7)**

Katie

**That means OR-06 is a MUST-HOLD for Democrats in 2026.** And it means we absolutely CANNOT afford to give any ground in this district if we want to have ANY chance of flipping the House blue on Election Day.
**So I’m reaching out personally to ask:**_ _**[Can you pitch in any amount before midnight to help me hold OR-06 on our path to flipping the House blue in 2026? name: Martin's iPhone
subject: Hey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
Hey, My team just let me know we’re still 81% to our mid-quarter goal. This is a problem because where we land determines the resources we have for the rest of the quarter. clean_body: If we keep this up, she doesn’t stand a chance... | Peter, The outpouring of grassroots support I’ve received since officially announcing my gubernatorial campaign has been truly incredible. Within just a few hours of my announcement, we raised over $1 MILLION for my gubernatorial campaign and the New York GOP! Those kinds of numbers will send a shiver down Kathy Hochul’s spine. The worst governor in America knows she can’t win a free and fair election that’s decided on the issues. Her only hope is to BUY her way to reelection – so if we can keep this momentum going, she won’t stand a chance! But that last part is key, Peter... To compete with the Democrats’ multimillion-dollar ad buys and win this race, we have to keep our momentum going from today all the way through Election Day. That’s why I set an ambitious goal of raising an additional $1,000,000 by midnight tomorrow to ensure we close out our launch week in the strongest possible position. Let’s CRUSH this goal and show Hochul that her days in office are numbered! Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801 |

It feels like November is flying by.
We’re already halfway through the month. **But we’re _not_ halfway to our November fundraising goal. We’re $44,500 behind where we should be right now.**
I’m no stranger to winning competitive elections. I know it takes hard work and A LOT of resources. With John Sununu entering the race and Roll Call downgrading our chances of winning, we must demonstrate that we have the resources to win this race. Now is not the time to fall behind.
Right now I’m asking this team to dig deep and help us close this $44,500 gap before midnight tonight. National Republicans and GOP super PACs are preparing to flood New Hampshire with millions of dollars. We can’t miss our goal — **we have to prove that New Hampshire can’t be bought.**
**[Will you chip in $5 or any amount to stand with me in the fight?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251115_1_long.dK8XqW&refcode=em20251115-1&t=2&akid=3499%2E582294%2EWyMiBC)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251115_1_long.dK8XqW&amount=5&refcode=em20251115-1&t=21&akid=3499%2E582294%2EWyMiBC)**
With gratitude,
Chris
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251115-1&t=22&refcode2=3499_582294_WyMiBC&akid=3499%2E582294%2EWyMiBC)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=3499%2E582294%2EWyMiBC)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)

name: Florida is GOING AFTER Planned Parenthood [via Fight For Reform]
subject: we need 50,000 SIGNATURES from your state..will you sign??
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg) [**DISGUSTING UPDATE:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=3&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg) [**Florida is SUING Planned Parenthood for $350 MILLION and seeking to shut down the organization altogether!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=4&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg) **Team, we’re appalled! So we’re launching an emergency petition to STAND with Planned Parenthood against Florida’s extremist Republican leaders.**

[**If we can get 50,000 signatures from your state before MIDNIGHT, our petition will go VIRAL. Florida Republicans won’t be able to ignore the public outrage over their disgusting acts! Please, team, add your signature to our petition now >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=5&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg) | [ADD MY NAME: STAND WITH PLANNED PARENTHOOD >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=6&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg)
---
**** This petition is for you only. Please don’t ignore this incredibly important request.** [**Your signature is due by 11:59 PM! ****](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=7&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg)
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=8&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg)
Team — this latest assault on Planned Parenthood and women’s health is disgusting.

Florida Republicans are now suing Planned Parenthood for **racketeering** — _accusing them of operating like an organized crime ring._ SHAME! ON! THEM!

Florida already has some of the most extreme abortion bans in the country, and this lawsuit is just their newest attempt to further restrict abortion access and essential healthcare for women.

If we can show MASSIVE public outrage over this, we can pressure Florida Republicans to drop this ridiculous, politically motivated case. **YES!!!**

So we’re circulating a CRUCIAL petition STANDING with Planned Parenthood, and we need you to add your voice:
[ **50,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED IN YOUR STATE: Please, sign our petition NOW to STAND with Planned Parenthood against these attacks >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=9&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg)
[ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70050?t=10&akid=23620%2E4449941%2EjHQLQg)
---
We know we can count on you,

name: Elizabeth
subject: 🚨 Reproductive rights are on the line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | Friend — On Tuesday, November 4, Virginia made history. We won our race _by 10 points (!)_ , and Democrats made a clean sweep in Richmond! A fantastic night. But now everything is on the line, once again. _And it’s up to us to finish the fight._ Right now, lawmakers in Richmond are advancing a **constitutional amendment to protect reproductive freedom** — a once-in-a-generation chance to **guarantee that every Virginian can make their own decisions about their reproductive health and pregnancy and childbirth,****_free from political interference._** If this amendment passes the General Assembly in 2026, it then goes straight to the voters for ratification. **_That means the next move belongs to us._** I’m ready to fight for this every single day in the General Assembly — but I need to know you’re with me. ******[Will you add your name right now to tell the Virginia Assembly to vote YES for reproductive freedom?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39VWni4g-3yZ6VJV1ZlrCSTMfv0ZYNVNgsSb9Eb0vP48ZaSw3f-JdJ1CY2pRl1sTqaymaoj_7FF9rTikP4H1lNOfqpemvabuKKF1vgn92hw9gQZz7S_efXdaUNQtEMUSwJpiqHfwsuyUfWOf6z5Rm4PQOjyETs7T8uIs1gnPm19lNBks6qLL_Cd234_V34yXoP_Yfb6yK4kKRtaIYChaXyWFTF6RF3E2cq4J8pqL1x6zN35qLCc62IuTkCCgk3a7JgzfXmotBAqQeMhqpnoY24uV2e8JOGhCZSClMnl0llNrQ/4lm/mygFXq70TS6BAJvu6MO5DA/h0/h001.Df213J4eAbkkzd7tOIv-e9OqgYnt7GJPxtYGk-av4NI)** [SIGN THE PETITION TODAY](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39VWni4g-3yZ6VJV1ZlrCSTMfv0ZYNVNgsSb9Eb0vP48ZaSw3f-JdJ1CY2pRl1sTqaymaoj_7FF9rTikP4H1lNOfqpemvabuKKF1vgn92hw9prZLaNYRjIKWd5ecKDhE6dcEoXF_1YuHkHVrOegPllUpABTCpTfwHhNTi_QIOZtFxxowuLpa_5fM9lK8QUSVBxYTZwbFKXdhz2J60IpEyHtKMcaVXDLrkZF4-jHiO41S4BituVOtnVfV3-NNwB9ifIkAst2lSGz0FXGUw3cwyOqrjyKhJR-ZUwQSyWnfEN0jA/4lm/mygFXq70TS6BAJvu6MO5DA/h1/h001.b6VtrzeI4AxIoWK_t78S2yo1nuzfCgsYOCy2x0Q7uOY) Your support today is so important. You can bet anti-abortion extremists are already plotting to stop this amendment. They will flood social media with disinformation, rally their base, and push politicians to stall this amendment before it ever reaches the ballot. We can’t let them rewrite Virginia’s future. This is about control — about who gets to make decisions about our own bodies and our own futures. And I’ll be honest: I’ve had enough of politicians thinking they know better than we do. ******[This is our moment to say, loud and clear: Reproductive rights is human rights. Period.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39VWni4g-3yZ6VJV1ZlrCSTMfv0ZYNVNgsSb9Eb0vP48ZaSw3f-JdJ1CY2pRl1sTqaymaoj_7FF9rTikP4H1lNOfqpemvabuKKF1vgn92hw9srx78XeFzed_tn_ZlKSQizwdK0eEKfIo82_h95y1L2bidZBF_pqcMIj_jQXIwCA8YEIAAIFNDcpa4eCcV0y5R-F7Hik5HLukKdRUHOizdLu2y6BSsNdiHLPqsTK7_OCyzzt_LzH50oTa9vvOvZ5hVylRia-rSIaf_kEwiEbCeYypZiMygmpD0y3l4hE4DX-DQ/4lm/mygFXq70TS6BAJvu6MO5DA/h2/h001.H7STptOutksIKV1Yed4HgZoxoUZHulveXXiXWzilOio)** Let’s make this permanent. clean_body: | | | Friend — On Tuesday, November 4, Virginia made history. We won our race by 10 points (!) , and Democrats made a clean sweep in Richmond! A fantastic night. But now everything is on the line, once again. And it’s up to us to finish the fight. Right now, lawmakers in Richmond are advancing a constitutional amendment to protect reproductive freedom — a once-in-a-generation chance to guarantee that every Virginian can make their own decisions about their reproductive health and pregnancy and childbirth,free from political interference. If this amendment passes the General Assembly in 2026, it then goes straight to the voters for ratification. That means the next move belongs to us. I’m ready to fight for this every single day in the General Assembly — but I need to know you’re with me. **** Your support today is so important. You can bet anti-abortion extremists are already plotting to stop this amendment. They will flood social media with disinformation, rally their base, and push politicians to stall this amendment before it ever reaches the ballot. We can’t let them rewrite Virginia’s future. This is about control — about who gets to make decisions about our own bodies and our own futures. And I’ll be honest: I’ve had enough of politicians thinking they know better than we do. **** Let’s make this permanent. Let’s make it law. Thanks for being in this fight with me, — Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. Contributions or gifts to Guzman for Congress are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . subject: Switching places with a Navy SEAL

body:
I’ve led men when the margin between life and death was measured in seconds. | [](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=cc4346aa3eba4d1ab211bc112ffe209b&_e=T5TufyvpqOWV5lGtKFaKBtV7eWpMts10wOz4Tua0uvudqlT0En6gPN7jlw8AUKL9JK87c9j1wH7LkfXkJQK9-fEbUSFcf3mR1jyVZmXURwidIeTmrTJ0fzzg4YO3HheodhgrUoNGUIdEV3Tny75r60qZBJkSM_ATzFjqQv1V01JD-udd5LbWrhUT3IFqLixnyBzYYzeJ5A6AOpb6OGNvGBz3ywKc4C3UCWRvu2KKe8VNqBGmoyH9OIhKLVLErD0R3dKKnP-Q-53xNGRW5iWDYuXFTOWlnrLlvy7fAG_R1buF-VFJpf3zI5dVG5KmryN0ChJgzwT0S1sDqM_j7ohRDId_PHjtZXZaCqbPE8hSq7ge3EtvGT4H6Yp-kulTpGL0t8T-R-k8CjgMjzDBzaoFetpZ50sNsmoL2YOVW9Nmn5BgeA960kTa-GSyBLjIRowJUBuOm_DU8LL1ZJwDp7h7iqwyrKrBlynSqwdeeXCaWnzbofZtkYEt-3HpMCx4WUar3TJhKnJfQWp1gs4A0z6FdPf3IlYRN3c0NgYADkEagReKj1y4SkNp6JjZ-dHVYh-js4BIoFpN85USlYZULUv35ZMI4rHh5m9WP3Y5kmiPCufwYauuENzEfOYkcf1xd_PwQb5N6HHzs6JHv6lFfDWHwqn6SuOUYgenymW0cwkbE5UbEqpzCkh8gfQUpeflKlQOamiMB9Kd0hZ3mb5u8IdWry1QmMrVHIOVdcG3hZTUvyg%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

Imagine, for a moment, **switching shoes with an active-duty Navy SEAL.**

The satellite phone rang at 3:00 a.m. local time.

You’re in a 20-foot shipping container turned command post, somewhere in the Horn of Africa you never see on a map.

_**No staff. No teleprompter. No second chances.**_

One of our teams was pinned down.

Bad guys closing in.

Decision time: **extract now and risk the helo, or hold and risk the men.**

You make the call. You get every American home.

_**That’s what a real 3 A.M. decision feels like.**_

_[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=cc4346aa3eba4d1ab211bc112ffe209b&_e=T5TufyvpqOWV5lGtKFaKBtV7eWpMts10wOz4Tua0uvudqlT0En6gPN7jlw8AUKL9JK87c9j1wH7LkfXkJQK9-fEbUSFcf3mR1jyVZmXURwidIeTmrTJ0fzzg4YO3HheodhgrUoNGUIdEV3Tny75r60qZBJkSM_ATzFjqQv1V01JD-udd5LbWrhUT3IFqLixnyBzYYzeJ5A6AOpb6OGNvGBz3ywKc4C3UCWRvu2KKe8VNqBGmoyH9OIhKLVLErD0RfqMMzcIernnJUkT6h16sQ8UIeggRuJftDmApY7Ev7EBrL4vo24y3dAGqwVaTFHXxY3HJiHSmhUfxOE7Ls8zy5-w2AD2tBFc79OAZ0pK2MMKaWXxxyWOwc9tzsPdauYi89TK5DwqKjNq92VdPK6NFRWCavkVKAzh0cyWPgQ_Ck_oP7cJ3m47VNY3R3Q2xPUwVz6WKy2AJ_Re8aTOsMQTUCNyqTjYErbuT7LJeXVKbK4wXI2InCDnt3y-3PTb_hpWIOmyNgvQ_P9PkJCqVNUcCaOJ7vLpP_gtiiJoIXR9pO43y8jte2imv9gOKgOLsgyoIWlM4C4Zc9pXOnbw5ir5gVRxBMDYfUdrFey0BRJ42Rdkip9u_0LALfdp1PdaDPvhJhheQ_28yo6vYaAJQ_O0PNGAV8jVQlhpTLQI8twirEIXqXeGZl6aJ7MNkhEu31KI0dX2szzzzsy0SQqLn1KW4FkoPH5hvjpTlbtHPDiS-reM%3D)_
Now, Minnesota faces its own 3 A.M. moments—only the phone is ringing in **Minneapolis, St. Cloud, Duluth.**

1. **_A 74% spike in carjackings since 2020._**

2. **_Illegals with criminal records released into our neighborhoods because Walz-Flanagan sued to defund ICE detention._**

3. **_Small-town hospitals are closing because state mandates crushed rural budgets._**

But this isn’t just happening in Minnesota - it’s happening all across the country. In big cities, small towns, and in state capitals, with out-of-touch career politicians leading the charge.

_**That’s why I’m running for the U.S. Senate - because Americans need a battle-tested former Navy SEAL, like me, ready to take the tough 3:00 a.m. calls that are commonplace at the highest levels of government. **_

[**SEND A SEAL TO THE SENATE →**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=cc4346aa3eba4d1ab211bc112ffe209b&_e=T5TufyvpqOWV5lGtKFaKBtV7eWpMts10wOz4Tua0uvudqlT0En6gPN7jlw8AUKL9JK87c9j1wH7LkfXkJQK9-fEbUSFcf3mR1jyVZmXURwidIeTmrTJ0fzzg4YO3HheodhgrUoNGUIdEV3Tny75r60qZBJkSM_ATzFjqQv1V01JD-udd5LbWrhUT3IFqLixnyBzYYzeJ5A6AOpb6OGNvGBz3ywKc4C3UCWRvu2KKe8VNqBGmoyH9OIhKLVLErD0RsW1RNF_YRm2BcGtkm8aGh2nJF8DQj9w0uaxnAyR2ZZ6rfWVGGB_GCrvL2Qna0ih5D8BiwVl3H-MYuFOaxJHIYQJtbZlqehiIcm4bTBSHLKO4d2uJQfQqx45KFT74EN2xCVe41cSKuz5j9MEYAnHN1krdykXrL_jNpF3CgYxXansxK72Dwo6FCC_eW_DpvBvBvN6cfJbJl7MFwi0BcGbRZlfKHFKGZHVVXBY1ARZTu3nXi1QMBTHppHIog8id3GKlq38YR0Uj6oVDWvPRYe56cALZnClqyJi5dVDssJMtlp0nEa8rMK-LtjjV64zcGafHlvk29VI6PnQb1QE6iX9Via9Yn7y5Bt8T72uj9EYk7xfek19UP4XBePQMBN9aM_9ujDZ4unPVyecyLTUt1JuE_QhwKYPM0tfG_LrOgSEUTbmUQPYaq9SsNMDfsx3cT_egbXw68phR6BOQBxphOK11-rvZJPURi_1HOPOSIf7hJRM%3D)

My opponent?

She’s never taken a 3 A.M. call that didn’t come with a donor list attached.

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan. I’ve done **nine combat deployments** across **70 countries**. I’ve led men when the margin between life and death was measured in seconds.

Minnesota doesn’t need another career politician who reads a poll at 3:00 A.M.

America needs another Senator who has _****already answered the 3 A.M. call****_ —and brought everyone home.

The left’s most recent internal poll shows a _** **generic Republican within 2 points**. That means **this seat is flippable****_—but only with the help of grassroots conservatives like yourself. You must step up now if we’re to have any chance of flipping this seat for the first time since 2008.

Could you **[please consider contributing $5, $10, or even $25 to our campaign today?](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=cc4346aa3eba4d1ab211bc112ffe209b&_e=T5TufyvpqOWV5lGtKFaKBtV7eWpMts10wOz4Tua0uvudqlT0En6gPN7jlw8AUKL9JK87c9j1wH7LkfXkJQK9-fEbUSFcf3mR1jyVZmXURwidIeTmrTJ0fzzg4YO3HheodhgrUoNGUIdEV3Tny75r60qZBJkSM_ATzFjqQv1V01JD-udd5LbWrhUT3IFqLixnyBzYYzeJ5A6AOpb6OGNvGBz3ywKc4C3UCWRvu2KKe8VNqBGmoyH9OIhKLVLErD0RRYNt6J3u8p0kUDVSeIfGgXF6dNEgSTFOO7sbx-lQV8PfjNHKHRGYI9hMIB9aqrcr2mw2S3pWS0DSl81R3uHdw1-_g1WXfMT7L3Mq5iANMidmWGIV4QAr-q96Zu1Z49cFfMBjCRVIlKTqlmTh66cMdRyvsBJ3C5xD65K0UMBnXrgRSB8_o5AxgunMvGH62bNtAl0KAvUrRS0koOGpigcjOCkwVcsJCdwxcx8Mgzopf8RYUfhhrTQKURWOkXorleGVjrw_rXc4K0_3DhyNs_N7Brsrwg10yqdQ_1APTeCdT3ehXVSi91GI5ERjmsAL_dB-A9Nafj_EDWi4_R89dP8mnhJ_fr-vHbzxGKPAGSBanO1ZzMrTrpCjmben-Eg2bnQw_gsmQGwA5K7hz-D-23uKyxUnnsz-sshCEfXLYlDURPYW-E5jM8EzncRrbVCykseiR2Bqg0ld3OxZc4eLldd0L4noSaabFKNtSlr_buMPjSo%3D)** I have a story to tell and a lot of fight in me, but the one thing we don’t have is money.

[**SEND A SEAL TO THE SENATE →**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=cc4346aa3eba4d1ab211bc112ffe209b&_e=T5TufyvpqOWV5lGtKFaKBtV7eWpMts10wOz4Tua0uvudqlT0En6gPN7jlw8AUKL9JK87c9j1wH7LkfXkJQK9-fEbUSFcf3mR1jyVZmXURwidIeTmrTJ0fzzg4YO3HheodhgrUoNGUIdEV3Tny75r60qZBJkSM_ATzFjqQv1V01JD-udd5LbWrhUT3IFqLixnyBzYYzeJ5A6AOpb6OGNvGBz3ywKc4C3UCWRvu2KKe8VNqBGmoyH9OIhKLVLErD0R0BN8az3porTXs3acpLsRTAKvDlN5JUzTBBvF6q3S00vBVMZM7pQl0IOAIOfGDSxum5hC35zJkEaUgZkwgCLB7O43mtWH8PQF4wBcc-cIDarfAJAwUbMTxplxRKyirvq5nSfn1qrxiJZk7kswIzQ9mUpwomaCbylRytas6bWJTsJdnSx4IR8nffplszUWhvlcKVY1sBho0RK-Mo2VsxZ9s7L3C56J9aEBecWM78s2jlWsRz1tw_yA7MeAVWqoDLfukm-21R0sSji2njhKi3uoqihdH_bB_oFpUt92bP8QsCl7nhKNEkh0VjLXWayGqt8zDbkAGIMw9T9f1XCb3eGEzRhkklhN3Izqsr-skp_06CvWt_ZwMjwRZkw0IvyWDvG58wNEuca3UkXu5QetEPpu5KhfO-ZhTwLtL0Yd2WOa-u5Yh5rIn1HPCKpVM74-o1JnLAaGsw_4aoV0bYVfzjxpinAeAVPhpkQg_YrLmt-RK2E%3D)

I’m not backed by Hollywood or D.C. consultants. **I’m backed by conservatives like _you._******

Every dollar received by my campaign sends a message to the national GOP:_****Minnesota is in play. ****_ Because when the next 3 A.M. crisis hits—whether it’s a border surge, a riot, or a cyberattack— The American people deserve a U.S. Senator who’s **already been there**.

For a better tomorrow,
Adam Schwarze
Retired Navy SEAL
Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota
_**Former Navy SEAL officer
Marine Corps Veteran
Proud Minnesotan **_

clean_body: I’ve led men when the margin between life and death was measured in seconds. | Fellow Conservative, Imagine, for a moment, switching shoes with an active-duty Navy SEAL. The satellite phone rang at 3:00 a.m. local time. You’re in a 20-foot shipping container turned command post, somewhere in the Horn of Africa you never see on a map. No staff. No teleprompter. No second chances. One of our teams was pinned down. Bad guys closing in. Decision time: extract now and risk the helo, or hold and risk the men. You make the call. You get every American home. That’s what a real 3 A.M. decision feels like. Now, Minnesota faces its own 3 A.M. moments—only the phone is ringing in Minneapolis, St. Cloud, Duluth. 1. A 74% spike in carjackings since 2020. 2. Illegals with criminal records released into our neighborhoods because Walz-Flanagan sued to defund ICE detention. 3. Small-town hospitals are closing because state mandates crushed rural budgets. But this isn’t just happening in Minnesota - it’s happening all across the country. In big cities, small towns, and in state capitals, with out-of-touch career politicians leading the charge. That’s why I’m running for the U.S. Senate - because Americans need a battle-tested former Navy SEAL, like me, ready to take the tough 3:00 a.m. calls that are commonplace at the highest levels of government. My opponent? She’s never taken a 3 A.M. call that didn’t come with a donor list attached. I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan. I’ve done nine combat deployments across 70 countries. I’ve led men when the margin between life and death was measured in seconds. Minnesota doesn’t need another career politician who reads a poll at 3:00 A.M. America needs another Senator who has **already answered the 3 A.M. call —and brought everyone home. The left’s most recent internal poll shows a generic Republican within 2 points. That means this seat is flippable—but only with the help of grassroots conservatives like yourself. You must step up now if we’re to have any chance of flipping this seat for the first time since 2008. Could you I have a story to tell and a lot of fight in me, but the one thing we don’t have is money. I’m not backed by Hollywood or D.C. consultants. I’m backed by conservatives like you.**** Every dollar received by my campaign sends a message to the national GOP:Minnesota is in play. Because when the next 3 A.M. crisis hits—whether it’s a border surge, a riot, or a cyberattack— The American people deserve a U.S. Senator who’s already been there. For a better tomorrow, Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA** | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine.
